supported_here: F30 stores one narrow post-H54 stop/go bundle for minimal useful-kernel carryover.
F30 fixes H55 as the only follow-up packet and R60 as the only next runtime candidate if activated later.
F30 keeps R61 and H56 as the only later sequence while leaving F27, R53, and R54 blocked.
unsupported_here: F30 does not replace H54 as the current active docs-only packet.
F30 does not reopen arbitrary C, broad Wasm, transformed entry, or trainable entry.
F30 does not itself execute a runtime lane or claim bounded value.
disconfirmed_here: The idea that exact toy compiled-boundary evidence alone justifies broader compiled-execution claims without one minimal useful-kernel carryover test.
